ogTitle: Hampton Park vs Hansonville: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hampton Park and Hansonville.
heading: Hampton Park vs Hansonville — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hampton Park, Victoria and Hansonville, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hampton Park has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Hansonville (31/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hampton Park or Hansonville safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hampton Park has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Hansonville scores 35/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hampton Park scores 24/100 for bushfire risk. Hansonville scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hampton Park or Hansonville?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hampton Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
